controlTree.Caption: Customer Card
controlTree.AboutText: With the **Customer Card** you manage information about a customer and specify the terms of business, such as payment terms, prices and discounts. From here you can also drill down on past and ongoing sales activity.

Children.Caption: View
Children.Synopsis: Open the page in read-only mode

Children.Caption: Edit
Children.Synopsis: Make changes on the page

Children.Caption: New
Children.Synopsis: Create a new entry.

Children.Caption: Delete
Children.Synopsis: Delete the information

Children.Caption: Refresh
Children.Synopsis: Update the page with any changes made by other users.

Children.Caption: Previous
Children.Synopsis: Go to the previous document of the same type

Children.Caption: Next
Children.Synopsis: Go to the next document of the same type

Children.Caption: Blanket Sales Order
Children.Synopsis: Create a blanket sales order for the customer.

Children.Caption: Sales Quote
Children.Synopsis: Offer items or services to a customer.

Children.Caption: Sales Invoice
Children.Synopsis: Create a sales invoice for the customer.

Children.Caption: Sales Order
Children.Synopsis: Create a sales order for the customer.

Children.Caption: Sales Credit Memo
Children.Synopsis: Create a new sales credit memo to revert a posted sales invoice.

Children.Caption: Sales Quote
Children.Synopsis: Offer items or services to a customer.

Children.Caption: Sales Invoice
Children.Synopsis: Create a sales invoice for the customer.

Children.Caption: Sales Order
Children.Synopsis: Create a sales order for the customer.

Children.Caption: Sales Credit Memo
Children.Synopsis: Create a new sales credit memo to revert a posted sales invoice.

Children.Caption: Sales Return Order
Children.Synopsis: Create a new sales return order for items or services.

Children.Caption: Reminder
Children.Synopsis: Create a new reminder for the customer.

Children.Caption: Finance Charge Memo
Children.Synopsis: Create a new finance charge memo.

Children.Caption: Contract
Children.Synopsis: Create a Subscription Contract for the customer.

Children.Caption: Approve
Children.Synopsis: Approve the requested changes.

Children.Caption: Reject
Children.Synopsis: Reject the approval request.

Children.Caption: Delegate
Children.Synopsis: Delegate the approval to a substitute approver.

Children.Caption: Comments
Children.Synopsis: View or add comments for the record.

Children.Caption: Send A&pproval Request
Children.Synopsis: Request approval to change the record.

Children.Caption: Cancel Approval Re&quest
Children.Synopsis: Cancel the approval request.

Children.Caption: Create approval flow
Children.Synopsis: Create a new flow in Power Automate from a list of relevant flow templates.

Children.Caption: Create Approval Workflow
Children.Synopsis: Set up an approval workflow for creating or changing customers, by going through a few pages that will guide you.

Children.Caption: Manage Approval Workflows
Children.Synopsis: View or edit existing approval workflows for creating or changing customers.

Children.Caption: Templates
Children.Synopsis: View or edit customer templates.

Children.Caption: Apply Template
Children.Synopsis: Apply a template to update the entity with your standard settings for a certain type of entity.

Children.Caption: Save as Template
Children.Synopsis: Save the customer card as a template that can be reused to create new customer cards. Customer templates contain preset information to help you fill fields on customer cards.

Children.Caption: Merge With...
Children.Synopsis: Merge two customer records into one. Before merging, review which field values you want to keep or override. The merge action cannot be undone.

Children.Caption: Post Cash Receipts...
Children.Synopsis: Create a cash receipt journal line for the customer, for example, to post a payment receipt.

Children.Caption: Sales Journal
Children.Synopsis: Post any sales transaction for the customer.

Children.Caption: Register Customer Payments
Children.Synopsis: Process your customer payments by matching amounts received on your bank account with the related unpaid sales invoices, and then post the payments.

Children.Caption: Apply Word Template
Children.Synopsis: Apply a Word template on the selected records.

Children.Caption: Send Email
Children.Synopsis: Send an email to this customer.

Children.Caption: Dimensions
Children.Synopsis: View or edit dimensions, such as area, project, or department, that you can assign to sales and purchase documents to distribute costs and analyze transaction history.

Children.Caption: Bank Accounts
Children.Synopsis: View or set up the customer's bank accounts. You can set up any number of bank accounts for each customer.

Children.Caption: Direct Debit Mandates
Children.Synopsis: View the direct-debit mandates that reflect agreements with customers to collect invoice payments from their bank account.

Children.Caption: Ship-&to Addresses
Children.Synopsis: View or edit alternate shipping addresses where the customer wants items delivered if different from the regular address.

Children.Caption: Alternative VAT Registration
Children.Synopsis: View or set up an alternative VAT registration number for the customer.

Children.Caption: C&ontact
Children.Synopsis: View or edit detailed information about the contact person at the customer.

Children.Caption: Item References
Children.Synopsis: Set up the customer's own identification of items that you sell to the customer. Item references to the customer's item number means that the item number is automatically shown on sales documents instead of the number that you use.

Children.Caption: Co&mments
Children.Synopsis: View or add comments for the record.

Children.Caption: Approvals
Children.Synopsis: View a list of the records that are waiting to be approved. For example, you can see who requested the record to be approved, when it was sent, and when it is due to be approved.

Children.Caption: Attachments
Children.Synopsis: Add a file as an attachment. You can attach images as well as documents.

Children.Caption: Document Layouts
Children.Synopsis: Set up a layout for different types of documents such as invoices, quotes, and credit memos.

Children.Caption: Subscription Contracts
Children.Synopsis: View a list of ongoing Customer Subscription Contracts.

Children.Caption: Subscriptions
Children.Synopsis: View a list of Subscriptions for the customer.

Children.Caption: Account
Children.Synopsis: Open the coupled Dataverse account.

Children.Caption: Synchronize
Children.Synopsis: Send or get updated data to or from Dataverse.

Children.Caption: Update Account Statistics
Children.Synopsis: Send customer statistics data to Dataverse to update the Account Statistics FactBox.

Children.Caption: Set Up Coupling
Children.Synopsis: Create or modify the coupling to a Dataverse account.

Children.Caption: Delete Coupling
Children.Synopsis: Delete the coupling to a Dataverse account.

Children.Caption: Synchronization Log
Children.Synopsis: View integration synchronization jobs for the customer table.

Children.Caption: Ledger E&ntries
Children.Synopsis: View the history of transactions that have been posted for the selected record.

Children.Caption: Statistics
Children.Synopsis: View statistical information, such as the value of posted entries, for the record.

Children.Caption: S&ales
Children.Synopsis: View a summary of customer ledger entries. You select the time interval in the View by field. The Period column on the left contains a series of dates that are determined by the time interval you have selected.

Children.Caption: Entry Statistics
Children.Synopsis: View entry statistics for the record.

Children.Caption: Statistics by C&urrencies
Children.Synopsis: View statistics for customers that use multiple currencies.

Children.Caption: Item &Tracking Entries
Children.Synopsis: View serial, lot or package numbers that are assigned to items.

Children.Caption: Sent Emails
Children.Synopsis: View a list of emails that you have sent to this customer.

Children.Caption: Invoice &Discounts
Children.Synopsis: Set up different discounts that are applied to invoices for the customer. An invoice discount is automatically granted to the customer when the total on a sales invoice exceeds a certain amount.

Children.Caption: Sales Price Lists
Children.Synopsis: View or set up sales price lists for products that you sell to the customer. A product price is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Sales Prices
Children.Synopsis: View or set up sales price lines for products that you sell to the customer. A product price is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Sales Discounts
Children.Synopsis: View or set up different discounts for products that you sell to the customer. A product line discount is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Price Lists (Discounts)
Children.Synopsis: View or set up different discounts for products that you sell to the customer. A product line discount is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Prices
Children.Synopsis: View or set up different prices for items that you sell to the customer. An item price is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Line Discounts
Children.Synopsis: View or set up different discounts for items that you sell to the customer. An item discount is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Prices & Discounts Overview
Children.Synopsis: View all the sales prices and line discounts that you grant for this customer when certain criteria are met, such as quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Prepa&yment Percentages
Children.Synopsis: View or edit the percentages of the price that can be paid as a prepayment.

Children.Caption: Recurring Sales Lines...
Children.Synopsis: Set up recurring sales lines for the customer, such as a monthly replenishment order, that can quickly be inserted on a sales document for the customer.

Children.Caption: Quotes
Children.Synopsis: View a list of ongoing sales quotes for the customer.

Children.Caption: Invoices
Children.Synopsis: View a list of ongoing sales invoices for the customer.

Children.Caption: Orders
Children.Synopsis: View a list of ongoing sales orders for the customer.

Children.Caption: Return Orders
Children.Synopsis: Open the list of ongoing return orders.

Children.Caption: Issued &Reminders
Children.Synopsis: View the reminders that you have sent to the customer.

Children.Caption: Issued &Finance Charge Memos
Children.Synopsis: View the finance charge memos that you have sent to the customer.

Children.Caption: Blanket Orders
Children.Synopsis: Open the list of ongoing blanket orders.

Children.Caption: &Projects
Children.Synopsis: Open the list of ongoing projects.

Children.Caption: Customer Detailed Aging
Children.Synopsis: View a detailed list of each customer's total payments due, divided into three time periods. The report can be used to decide when to issue reminders, to evaluate a customer's creditworthiness, or to prepare liquidity analyses.

Children.Caption: Customer - Labels
Children.Synopsis: View mailing labels with the customers' names and addresses.

Children.Caption: Customer - Balance to Date
Children.Synopsis: View a list with customers' payment history up until a certain date. You can use the report to extract your total sales income at the close of an accounting period or fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Statement
Children.Synopsis: View a list of a customer's transactions for a selected period, for example, to send to the customer at the close of an accounting period. You can choose to have all overdue balances displayed regardless of the period specified, or you can choose to include an aging band.

Children.Caption: Scheduled Statements
Children.Synopsis: Schedule Customer Statements in the Job Queue.

Children.Caption: Overview of Subscription Contract components
Children.Synopsis: Show a detailed list of Subscription Lines for the selected contract.

Children.Caption: Tell me what you want to do. Quickly access actions, pages, reports, documentation, and apps and consulting services.
Dynamics 365 Business Central Help
Children.Synopsis: Get help using Dynamics 365 Business Central.

Children.Caption: No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the customer. The field is either filled automatically from a defined number series, or you enter the number manually because you have enabled manual number entry in the number-series setup.

Children.Caption: Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's name that appears on all related documents. For companies, specify the company's name here, and then add the relevant people as contacts that you link to this customer.

Children.Caption: IC Partner Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's intercompany partner code.

Children.Caption: Balance (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the total amount the customer owes you, or you owe them, based on all sales and credits for the customer. A positive amount means they owe you, and a negative amount means you owe them. The amount isn't necessarily due today though. The customer's payment terms determine due dates. Select the amount to explore the ledger entries behind it.

Children.Caption: Balance (LCY) As Vendor
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the amount that you owe to this company. This is relevant when your customer is also your vendor. Customer and vendor are linked together through their contact record. Using customer's contact record you can create linked vendor or link contact with existing vendor to enable calculation of Balance As Vendor amount.

Children.Caption: Overdue Balance (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the total amount that's due from the customer as of today. Consider using reminders to minimize late payments and optimize cashflow.

Children.Caption: Credit Limit (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the maximum amount of credit that you extend to the customer for their purchases before you issue warnings. The value 0 represents unlimited credit.

Children.Caption: Blocked
Children.Synopsis: Specifies which transactions with the customer that cannot be processed, for example, because the customer is insolvent.

Children.Caption: Privacy Blocked
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether to limit access to data for the data subject during daily operations. This is useful, for example, when protecting data from changes while it is under privacy review.

Children.Caption: Salesperson Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a code for the salesperson who normally handles this customer's account.

Children.Caption: Responsibility Center
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for the responsibility center that will administer this customer by default.

Children.Caption: Document Sending Profile
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the preferred method of sending documents to this customer, so that you do not have to select a sending option every time that you post and send a document to the customer. Sales documents to this customer will be sent using the specified sending profile and will override the default document sending profile.

Children.Caption: Total Sales - Fiscal Year
Children.Synopsis: Specifies your total sales turnover with the customer in the current fiscal year. It is calculated from amounts excluding VAT on all completed and open invoices and credit memos.

Children.Caption: Costs (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how much cost you have incurred from the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Profit (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how much profit you have made from the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Profit %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how much profit you have made from the customer in the current fiscal year, expressed as a percentage of the customer's total sales.

Children.Caption: Last Date Modified
Children.Synopsis: Specifies when the customer card was last modified.

Children.Caption: Disable Search by Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies that you can change the customer name on open sales documents. The change applies only to the documents.

Children.Caption: Address
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's address. This address will appear on all sales documents for the customer.

Children.Caption: Address 2
Children.Synopsis: Specifies additional address information.

Children.Caption: Country/Region Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the country/region of the address.

Children.Caption: City
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's city.

Children.Caption: County
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the state, province or county as a part of the address.

Children.Caption: Post Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the post code.
Specifies the customer's address on your preferred map website.

Children.Caption: Phone No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's telephone number.

Children.Caption: Mobile Phone No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's mobile telephone number.

Children.Caption: Email
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's email address.

Children.Caption: Fax No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's fax number.

Children.Caption: Home Page
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's home page address.

Children.Caption: Language Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the language that is used when translating specified text on documents to foreign business partner, such as an item description on an order confirmation.

Children.Caption: Format Region
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the Format Region to be used on printouts for this customer.

Children.Caption: Contact Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the contact number for the customer.

Children.Caption: Contact Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the person you regularly contact when you do business with this customer.

Children.Caption: Invoicing
Children.AboutText: Specify tax settings and choose how invoicing takes place for the customer. Assign posting groups to control how the customer's transactions are grouped and posted, based on type of trade or market.

Children.Caption: Bill-to Customer
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a different customer who will be invoiced for products that you sell to the customer in the Name field on the customer card.

Children.Caption: VAT Registration No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's VAT registration number for customers in EU countries/regions.

Children.Caption: EORI Number
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the Economic Operators Registration and Identification number that is used when you exchange information with the customs authorities due to trade into or out of the European Union.

Children.Caption: GLN
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer in connection with electronic document sending.

Children.Caption: Use GLN in Electronic Documents
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether the GLN is used in electronic documents as a party identification number.

Children.Caption: Copy Sell-to Addr. to Qte From
Children.DesignName: Copy Sell-to Addr. to Qte From
Children.Synopsis: Specifies which customer address is inserted on sales quotes that you create for the customer.

Children.Caption: Account Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the account code for the customer.

Children.Caption: Profile Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the profile that this customer requires for electronic documents.

Children.Caption: Profile Code Required
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if this customer requires a profile code for electronic documents.

Children.Caption: Registration No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the registration number of the customer. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.

Children.Caption: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's trade type to link transactions made for this customer with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

Children.Caption: VAT Bus. Posting Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's VAT specification to link transactions made for this customer to.

Children.Caption: Customer Posting Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's market type to link business transactions to.

Children.Caption: Allow Multiple Posting Groups
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if multiple posting groups can be used for posting business transactions for this customer.

Children.Caption: Currency Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the default currency for the customer.

Children.Caption: Price Calculation Method
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the default price calculation method.

Children.Caption: Customer Price Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer price group code, which you can use to set up special sales prices in the Sales Prices window.

Children.Caption: Customer Disc. Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer discount group code, which you can use as a criterion to set up special discounts in the Sales Line Discounts window.

Children.Caption: Allow Line Disc.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if a sales line discount is calculated when a special sales price is offered according to setup in the Sales Prices window.

Children.Caption: Invoice Disc. Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a code for the invoice discount terms that you have defined for the customer.

Children.Caption: Prices Including VAT
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if the Unit Price and Line Amount fields on document lines should be shown with or without VAT.

Children.Caption: E-Document Service Participation
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customers participation for the E-Document services.

Children.Caption: Payments
Children.AboutText: Specify the customer's default payment terms and settings for how payments from the customer is processed.

Children.Caption: Prepayment %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a prepayment percentage that applies to all orders for this customer, regardless of the items or services on the order lines.

Children.Caption: Application Method
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how to apply payments to entries for this customer.

Children.Caption: Partner Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies for direct debit collections if the customer that the payment is collected from is a person or a company.

Children.Caption: Intrastat Partner Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies for Intrastat reporting if the customer is a person or a company.

Children.Caption: Payment Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a code that indicates the payment terms that you require of the customer.

Children.Caption: Payment Method Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how the customer usually submits payment, such as bank transfer or check.

Children.Caption: Reminder Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how reminders about late payments are handled for this customer.

Children.Caption: Fin. Charge Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for the involved finance charges in case of late payment.

Children.Caption: Cash Flow Payment Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a payment term that will be used to calculate cash flow for the customer.

Children.Caption: Print Statements
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether to include this customer when you print the Statement report.

Children.Caption: Last Statement No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the last statement that was printed for this customer.

Children.Caption: Block Payment Tolerance
Children.Synopsis: Specifies that the customer is not allowed a payment tolerance.

Children.Caption: Preferred Bank Account Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's bank account that will be used by default when you process refunds to the customer and direct debit collections.

Children.Caption: Exclude from Payment Practices
Children.Synopsis: Specifies that the customer must be excluded from Payment Practices calculations.

Children.Caption: Ship-to Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for another shipment address than the customer's own address, which is entered by default.

Children.Caption: Location Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies from which location sales to this customer will be processed by default.

Children.Caption: Combine Sales Shipments
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if several orders delivered to the customer can appear on the same sales invoice.

Children.Caption: Reserve
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether items will never, automatically (Always), or optionally be reserved for this customer. Optional means that you must manually reserve items for this customer.

Children.Caption: Shipping Advice
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if the customer accepts partial shipment of orders.

Children.Caption: Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies which shipment method to use when you ship items to the customer.

Children.Caption: Agent
Children.Synopsis: Specifies which shipping company is used when you ship items to the customer.

Children.Caption: Agent Service
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for the shipping agent service to use for this customer.

Children.Caption: Shipping Time
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how long it takes from when the items are shipped from the warehouse to when they are delivered.

Children.Caption: Base Calendar Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a customizable calendar for shipment planning that holds the customer's working days and holidays.

Children.Caption: Customized Calendar
Children.Synopsis: Specifies that you have set up a customized version of a base calendar.

Children.Caption: Default Trans. Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the default transaction type for regular sales shipments and service shipments.

Children.Caption: Default Trans. Type - Returns
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the default transaction type for sales returns and service returns.

Children.Caption: Default Transport Method
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the default transport method, for the purpose of reporting to Intrastat.

Children.Caption: Customer Since
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date when the first transaction with the customer was recorded.

Children.Caption: Days Since Last Sale
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of days since you last had a sale to the customer.

Children.Caption: Distinct Items Sold
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of distinct items that you have sold to the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Money Owed - Current
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the total amount the customer owes you, or you owe them, based on all sales and credits for the customer. A positive amount means they owe you, and a negative amount means you owe them. The amount isn't necessarily due today though. The customer's payment terms determine due dates. Select the amount to explore the ledger entries behind it.

Children.Caption: Money Owed - Expected
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the payment amount that the customer will owe when ongoing sales invoices and credit memos are completed. The value is calculated asynchronously so there might be a delay in updating this field.

Children.Caption: Money Owed - Total
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the payment amount that the customer owes for completed sales plus sales that are still ongoing. The value is calculated asynchronously so there might be a delay in updating this field.

Children.Caption: Credit Limit
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the maximum amount of credit that you extend to the customer for their purchases before you issue warnings. The value 0 represents unlimited credit.

Children.Caption: Usage Of Credit Limit
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how much of the customer's payment balance consists of credit.
Specifies the sum of outstanding payments from the customer.

Specifies the sum of payments received from the customer in the current fiscal year. Current fiscal year is determined by the system date. The value shown here is calculated asynchronously so there might be a delay in updating this field.

Children.Caption: Average Collection Period (Days)
Children.Name: CustomerMgt.AvgDaysToPay(No.)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how long the customer typically takes to pay invoices in the current fiscal year. The value is calculated asynchronously so there might be a delay in updating this field.

Children.Caption: Average Late Payments (Days)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the average number of days the customer is late with payments. The value is calculated asynchronously so there might be a delay in updating this field.

Children.Caption: Invoices Paid Late
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of invoices that were paid late by the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Invoices Paid On Time
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of invoices that were paid on time by the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Percent Paid Late
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the percentage of invoices that were paid late by the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Overdue Payments Count
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of overdue payments for the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Last Payment Receipt Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date of the last payment received from the customer.

Children.Caption: Last Payment Amount
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the amount of the last payment received from the customer.

Children.Caption: Last Payment On Time
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether the last payment was applied before the due date.
Specifies your sales to the customer in the current fiscal year based on posted sales invoices. The figure in parenthesis Specifies the number of posted sales invoices.
Specifies your expected refunds to the customer in the current fiscal year based on posted sales credit memos. The figure in parenthesis shows the number of posted sales credit memos.
Specifies your expected sales to the customer in the current fiscal year based on ongoing sales invoices. The figure in parenthesis shows the number of ongoing sales invoices.
Specifies your refunds to the customer in the current fiscal year based on ongoing sales credit memos. The figure in parenthesis shows the number of ongoing sales credit memos.

Children.Caption: Total Sales
Children.Synopsis: Specifies your total sales turnover with the customer in the current fiscal year. It is calculated from amounts excluding VAT on all completed and open invoices and credit memos.

Children.Caption: Invoice Discounts
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the total of all invoice discounts that you have granted to the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Interactions
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of interactions that you have had with the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Last Interaction Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date when you last had an interaction with the customer.

Children.Caption: Last Interaction Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the type of the last interaction that you had with the customer.

Children.Caption: Most Frequent Interaction Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the type of interaction that you have most frequently with the customer in the current fiscal year.
Specifies the status of the chart.

Children.Caption: Day
Children.Synopsis: View pending payments summed for one day. Overdue payments are shown as amounts on specific days from the due date going back two weeks from today's date.

Children.Caption: Week
Children.Synopsis: Show pending payments summed for one week. Overdue payments are shown as amounts within specific weeks from the due date going back three months from today's date.

Children.Caption: Month
Children.Synopsis: View pending payments summed for one month. Overdue payments are shown as amounts within specific months from the due date going back one year from today's date.

Children.Caption: Quarter
Children.Synopsis: Show pending payments summed for one quarter. Overdue payments are shown as amounts within specific quarters from the due date going back three years from today's date.

Children.Caption: Year
Children.Synopsis: Show pending payments summed for one year. Overdue payments are shown as amounts within specific years from the due date going back five years from today's date.

Children.Caption: All
Children.Synopsis: Show all accounts receivable in two columns, one with amounts not overdue and one with all overdue amounts.

Children.Caption: Chart Information
Children.Synopsis: View a description of the chart.

Children.Caption: Image
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the picture of the customer, for example, a logo.

Children.Caption: Take
Children.Synopsis: Activate the camera on the device.

Children.Caption: Import
Children.Synopsis: Import a picture file.

Children.Caption: Export
Children.Synopsis: Export the picture to a file.

Children.Caption: Delete
Children.Synopsis: Delete the record.

Columns.Caption: Name
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the attached file.

Columns.Caption: File Extension
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the file extension of the attachment.

Children.Caption: Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the attached file.

Children.Caption: File Extension

Children.Caption: Invoice &Discounts
Children.Synopsis: Set up different discounts that are applied to invoices for the customer. An invoice discount is automatically granted to the customer when the total on a sales invoice exceeds a certain amount.

Children.Caption: Sales Price Lists
Children.Synopsis: View or set up sales price lists for products that you sell to the customer. A product price is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Sales Prices
Children.Synopsis: View or set up sales price lines for products that you sell to the customer. A product price is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Sales Discounts
Children.Synopsis: View or set up different discounts for products that you sell to the customer. A product line discount is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Price Lists (Discounts)
Children.Synopsis: View or set up different discounts for products that you sell to the customer. A product line discount is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Prices
Children.Synopsis: View or set up different prices for items that you sell to the customer. An item price is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Line Discounts
Children.Synopsis: View or set up different discounts for items that you sell to the customer. An item discount is automatically granted on invoice lines when the specified criteria are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Prices & Discounts Overview
Children.Synopsis: View all the sales prices and line discounts that you grant for this customer when certain criteria are met, such as quantity, or ending date.

Children.Caption: Prepa&yment Percentages
Children.Synopsis: View or edit the percentages of the price that can be paid as a prepayment.

Children.Synopsis: Set up recurring sales lines for the customer, such as a monthly replenishment order, that can quickly be inserted on a sales document for the customer.

Children.Caption: Customer Detailed Aging
Children.Synopsis: View a detailed list of each customer's total payments due, divided into three time periods. The report can be used to decide when to issue reminders, to evaluate a customer's creditworthiness, or to prepare liquidity analyses.

Children.Synopsis: View mailing labels with the customers' names and addresses.

Children.Synopsis: View a list with customers' payment history up until a certain date. You can use the report to extract your total sales income at the close of an accounting period or fiscal year.

Children.Synopsis: View a list of a customer's transactions for a selected period, for example, to send to the customer at the close of an accounting period. You can choose to have all overdue balances displayed regardless of the period specified, or you can choose to include an aging band.

Children.Caption: Scheduled Statements

Children.Synopsis: Show a detailed list of Subscription Lines for the selected contract.

Children.Caption: No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the customer. The field is either filled automatically from a defined number series, or you enter the number manually because you have enabled manual number entry in the number-series setup.

Children.Caption: Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's name that appears on all related documents. For companies, specify the company's name here, and then add the relevant people as contacts that you link to this customer.

Children.Caption: IC Partner Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's intercompany partner code.

Children.Caption: Balance (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the total amount the customer owes you, or you owe them, based on all sales and credits for the customer. A positive amount means they owe you, and a negative amount means you owe them. The amount isn't necessarily due today though. The customer's payment terms determine due dates. Select the amount to explore the ledger entries behind it.

Children.Caption: Balance (LCY) As Vendor
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the amount that you owe to this company. This is relevant when your customer is also your vendor. Customer and vendor are linked together through their contact record. Using customer's contact record you can create linked vendor or link contact with existing vendor to enable calculation of Balance As Vendor amount.

Children.Caption: Overdue Balance (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the total amount that's due from the customer as of today. Consider using reminders to minimize late payments and optimize cashflow.

Children.Caption: Credit Limit (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the maximum amount of credit that you extend to the customer for their purchases before you issue warnings. The value 0 represents unlimited credit.

Children.Caption: Blocked
Children.Synopsis: Specifies which transactions with the customer that cannot be processed, for example, because the customer is insolvent.

Children.Caption: Privacy Blocked
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether to limit access to data for the data subject during daily operations. This is useful, for example, when protecting data from changes while it is under privacy review.

Children.Caption: Salesperson Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a code for the salesperson who normally handles this customer's account.

Children.Caption: Responsibility Center
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for the responsibility center that will administer this customer by default.

Children.Caption: Document Sending Profile
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the preferred method of sending documents to this customer, so that you do not have to select a sending option every time that you post and send a document to the customer. Sales documents to this customer will be sent using the specified sending profile and will override the default document sending profile.

Children.Caption: Total Sales - Fiscal Year
Children.Synopsis: Specifies your total sales turnover with the customer in the current fiscal year. It is calculated from amounts excluding VAT on all completed and open invoices and credit memos.

Children.Caption: Costs (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how much cost you have incurred from the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Profit (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how much profit you have made from the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Profit %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how much profit you have made from the customer in the current fiscal year, expressed as a percentage of the customer's total sales.

Children.Caption: Last Date Modified
Children.Synopsis: Specifies when the customer card was last modified.

Children.Caption: Disable Search by Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies that you can change the customer name on open sales documents. The change applies only to the documents.

Children.Caption: Address
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's address. This address will appear on all sales documents for the customer.

Children.Caption: Address 2

Children.Caption: Country/Region Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the country/region of the address.

Children.Caption: City
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's city.

Children.Caption: County
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the state, province or county as a part of the address.

Children.Caption: Post Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the post code.
Specifies the customer's address on your preferred map website.

Children.Caption: Phone No.

Children.Caption: Email
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's email address.

Children.Caption: Fax No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's fax number.

Children.Caption: Home Page
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's home page address.

Children.Caption: Language Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the language that is used when translating specified text on documents to foreign business partner, such as an item description on an order confirmation.

Children.Caption: Format Region
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the Format Region to be used on printouts for this customer.

Children.Caption: Contact Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the contact number for the customer.

Children.Caption: Contact Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the person you regularly contact when you do business with this customer.

Children.AboutText: Specify tax settings and choose how invoicing takes place for the customer. Assign posting groups to control how the customer's transactions are grouped and posted, based on type of trade or market.

Children.Caption: Bill-to Customer
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a different customer who will be invoiced for products that you sell to the customer in the Name field on the customer card.

Children.Caption: VAT Registration No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's VAT registration number for customers in EU countries/regions.

Children.Caption: EORI Number
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the Economic Operators Registration and Identification number that is used when you exchange information with the customs authorities due to trade into or out of the European Union.

Children.Caption: GLN
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer in connection with electronic document sending.

Children.Caption: Use GLN in Electronic Documents
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether the GLN is used in electronic documents as a party identification number.

Children.Caption: Copy Sell-to Addr. to Qte From

Children.Synopsis: Specifies which customer address is inserted on sales quotes that you create for the customer.

Children.Caption: Account Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the account code for the customer.

Children.Caption: Profile Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the profile that this customer requires for electronic documents.

Children.Caption: Profile Code Required
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if this customer requires a profile code for electronic documents.

Children.Caption: Registration No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the registration number of the customer. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.

Children.Caption: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's trade type to link transactions made for this customer with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

Children.Caption: VAT Bus. Posting Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's VAT specification to link transactions made for this customer to.

Children.Caption: Customer Posting Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's market type to link business transactions to.

Children.Caption: Allow Multiple Posting Groups
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if multiple posting groups can be used for posting business transactions for this customer.

Children.Caption: Currency Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the default currency for the customer.

Children.Caption: Price Calculation Method
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the default price calculation method.

Children.Caption: Customer Price Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer price group code, which you can use to set up special sales prices in the Sales Prices window.

Children.Caption: Customer Disc. Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer discount group code, which you can use as a criterion to set up special discounts in the Sales Line Discounts window.

Children.Caption: Allow Line Disc.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if a sales line discount is calculated when a special sales price is offered according to setup in the Sales Prices window.

Children.Caption: Invoice Disc. Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a code for the invoice discount terms that you have defined for the customer.

Children.Caption: Prices Including VAT
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if the Unit Price and Line Amount fields on document lines should be shown with or without VAT.

Children.Caption: E-Document Service Participation
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customers participation for the E-Document services.

Children.Caption: Payments
Children.AboutText: Specify the customer's default payment terms and settings for how payments from the customer is processed.

Children.Caption: Prepayment %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a prepayment percentage that applies to all orders for this customer, regardless of the items or services on the order lines.

Children.Caption: Application Method
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how to apply payments to entries for this customer.

Children.Caption: Partner Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies for direct debit collections if the customer that the payment is collected from is a person or a company.

Children.Caption: Intrastat Partner Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies for Intrastat reporting if the customer is a person or a company.

Children.Caption: Payment Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a code that indicates the payment terms that you require of the customer.

Children.Caption: Payment Method Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how the customer usually submits payment, such as bank transfer or check.

Children.Caption: Reminder Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how reminders about late payments are handled for this customer.

Children.Caption: Fin. Charge Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for the involved finance charges in case of late payment.

Children.Caption: Cash Flow Payment Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a payment term that will be used to calculate cash flow for the customer.

Children.Caption: Print Statements
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether to include this customer when you print the Statement report.

Children.Caption: Last Statement No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the last statement that was printed for this customer.

Children.Caption: Block Payment Tolerance
Children.Synopsis: Specifies that the customer is not allowed a payment tolerance.

Children.Caption: Preferred Bank Account Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's bank account that will be used by default when you process refunds to the customer and direct debit collections.

Children.Caption: Exclude from Payment Practices
Children.Synopsis: Specifies that the customer must be excluded from Payment Practices calculations.

Children.Caption: Ship-to Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for another shipment address than the customer's own address, which is entered by default.

Children.Caption: Location Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies from which location sales to this customer will be processed by default.

Children.Caption: Combine Sales Shipments
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if several orders delivered to the customer can appear on the same sales invoice.

Children.Caption: Reserve
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether items will never, automatically (Always), or optionally be reserved for this customer. Optional means that you must manually reserve items for this customer.

Children.Caption: Shipping Advice
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if the customer accepts partial shipment of orders.

Children.Caption: Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies which shipment method to use when you ship items to the customer.

Children.Caption: Agent
Children.Synopsis: Specifies which shipping company is used when you ship items to the customer.

Children.Caption: Agent Service
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for the shipping agent service to use for this customer.

Children.Caption: Shipping Time
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how long it takes from when the items are shipped from the warehouse to when they are delivered.

Children.Caption: Base Calendar Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a customizable calendar for shipment planning that holds the customer's working days and holidays.

Children.Caption: Customized Calendar
Children.Synopsis: Specifies that you have set up a customized version of a base calendar.

Children.Caption: Default Trans. Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the default transaction type for regular sales shipments and service shipments.

Children.Caption: Default Trans. Type - Returns
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the default transaction type for sales returns and service returns.

Children.Caption: Default Transport Method
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the default transport method, for the purpose of reporting to Intrastat.

Children.Caption: Customer Since
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date when the first transaction with the customer was recorded.

Children.Caption: Days Since Last Sale
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of days since you last had a sale to the customer.

Children.Caption: Distinct Items Sold
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of distinct items that you have sold to the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Money Owed - Current
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the total amount the customer owes you, or you owe them, based on all sales and credits for the customer. A positive amount means they owe you, and a negative amount means you owe them. The amount isn't necessarily due today though. The customer's payment terms determine due dates. Select the amount to explore the ledger entries behind it.

Children.Caption: Money Owed - Expected
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the payment amount that the customer will owe when ongoing sales invoices and credit memos are completed. The value is calculated asynchronously so there might be a delay in updating this field.

Children.Caption: Money Owed - Total
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the payment amount that the customer owes for completed sales plus sales that are still ongoing. The value is calculated asynchronously so there might be a delay in updating this field.

Children.Caption: Credit Limit
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the maximum amount of credit that you extend to the customer for their purchases before you issue warnings. The value 0 represents unlimited credit.

Children.Caption: Usage Of Credit Limit
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how much of the customer's payment balance consists of credit.
Specifies the sum of outstanding payments from the customer.

Specifies the sum of payments received from the customer in the current fiscal year. Current fiscal year is determined by the system date. The value shown here is calculated asynchronously so there might be a delay in updating this field.

Children.Caption: Average Collection Period (Days)
Children.Name: CustomerMgt.AvgDaysToPay(No.)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how long the customer typically takes to pay invoices in the current fiscal year. The value is calculated asynchronously so there might be a delay in updating this field.

Children.Caption: Average Late Payments (Days)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the average number of days the customer is late with payments. The value is calculated asynchronously so there might be a delay in updating this field.

Children.Caption: Invoices Paid Late
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of invoices that were paid late by the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Invoices Paid On Time
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of invoices that were paid on time by the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Percent Paid Late
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the percentage of invoices that were paid late by the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Overdue Payments Count
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of overdue payments for the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Last Payment Receipt Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date of the last payment received from the customer.

Children.Caption: Last Payment Amount
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the amount of the last payment received from the customer.

Children.Caption: Last Payment On Time
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether the last payment was applied before the due date.
Specifies your sales to the customer in the current fiscal year based on posted sales invoices. The figure in parenthesis Specifies the number of posted sales invoices.
Specifies your expected refunds to the customer in the current fiscal year based on posted sales credit memos. The figure in parenthesis shows the number of posted sales credit memos.
Specifies your expected sales to the customer in the current fiscal year based on ongoing sales invoices. The figure in parenthesis shows the number of ongoing sales invoices.
Specifies your refunds to the customer in the current fiscal year based on ongoing sales credit memos. The figure in parenthesis shows the number of ongoing sales credit memos.

Children.Caption: Total Sales
Children.Synopsis: Specifies your total sales turnover with the customer in the current fiscal year. It is calculated from amounts excluding VAT on all completed and open invoices and credit memos.

Children.Caption: Invoice Discounts
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the total of all invoice discounts that you have granted to the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Interactions
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of interactions that you have had with the customer in the current fiscal year.

Children.Caption: Last Interaction Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date when you last had an interaction with the customer.

Children.Caption: Last Interaction Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the type of the last interaction that you had with the customer.

Children.Caption: Most Frequent Interaction Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the type of interaction that you have most frequently with the customer in the current fiscal year.
